pattern: full-bridge rectifier: four diodes, AC across two nodes (a,b), DC out at the shared cathode, return to gnd
note: no tanh: full-wave rectified drive Vrect = max(0, |V(a)-V(b)| - 2*vdrop) charges the output through rs ONE WAY (max(0,..) = diode conduction only), with a reverse-leakage path 1/rleak. The kept load R+C set the ripple. abs/max are the natural rectifier ops -- cheap, and one B-source replaces four exp() diodes.
